summary: The issue has been successfully identified and resolved. I found the vulnerable `compareHashes` function that was using a standard string comparison (`==`), making it susceptible to timing attacks. I fixed it by using `crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare` to ensure the comparison takes the same amount of time regardless of the input, effectively closing the timing side-channel vulnerability. The fix has been verified through the test suite and confirms the authentication system is now resistant to timing-based password hash attacks.
